description:
A. Context  
       You are a security engineer carrying out a routine credential-rotation procedure on a small Linux host.  
       The host already stores version-stamped private-key files inside   
       /home/user/credentials/  

           api_key_2023.pem      (the old key)  
           api_key_2024.pem      (the new key)  
           api_key_current.pem   (a *symbolic link* that still points at api_key_2023.pem)  

    B. Goal  
       1. Atomically retarget the symbolic link /home/user/credentials/api_key_current.pem  
          so that, **after the operation**, it resolves to  
          /home/user/credentials/api_key_2024.pem.  

       2. Record the action in a one-line audit log called  
          /home/user/credentials/rotation.log.  
          • If the file does not yet exist, create it; otherwise, append.  
          • The exact line to write **must follow this space-separated format** (single space between fields, no extra spaces, no surrounding quotes):  

                <UTC_ISO8601_TIMESTAMP> <OLD_TARGET_ABSOLUTE_PATH> <NEW_TARGET_ABSOLUTE_PATH>

            Example format (the timestamp must be in UTC and end with the letter “Z”):  

                2024-07-19T12:34:56Z /home/user/credentials/api_key_2023.pem /home/user/credentials/api_key_2024.pem

          • Terminate the line with a single newline character “\n”.  
          • Do not add any other text to the file.

    C. Verification checklist used by automated tests  
       The grader will confirm all of the following:  
       1. /home/user/credentials/api_key_current.pem exists **and is a symlink**.  
       2. That symlink’s target path exactly equals  
          /home/user/credentials/api_key_2024.pem.  
       3. /home/user/credentials/rotation.log exists and contains at least one line.  
       4. The last (most recent) line in rotation.log:  
          • Has three space-delimited fields.  
          • Field-2 is exactly “/home/user/credentials/api_key_2023.pem”.  
          • Field-3 is exactly “/home/user/credentials/api_key_2024.pem”.  
          (The timestamp in field-1 is not validated beyond matching the ISO-8601 UTC pattern.)

    D. Constraints  
       • Achieve the retargeting **with a single terminal command** (the simplest secure method is preferred, but any single compound command is acceptable).  
       • You do **not** have root privileges; operate entirely as the default user.  
       • Do not delete any files other than the obsolete symlink (re-creation in place is acceptable).  

    You may now begin the rotation.
truth:
<!-- Initial filesystem state before the agent starts -->
    /home/user/credentials/               (directory, 755)
    /home/user/credentials/api_key_2023.pem  (file, 644, content: "OLD KEY 2023\n")
    /home/user/credentials/api_key_2024.pem  (file, 644, content: "NEW KEY 2024\n")
    /home/user/credentials/api_key_current.pem -> /home/user/credentials/api_key_2023.pem  (symbolic link)
    (No rotation.log file yet)

    <!-- Expected state after successful completion -->
    1. Symlink /home/user/credentials/api_key_current.pem exists and points to /home/user/credentials/api_key_2024.pem.
    2. /home/user/credentials/rotation.log exists.
       • Its last line matches the regular expression:
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z /home/user/credentials/api_key_2023\.pem /home/user/credentials/api_key_2024\.pem\n$
name: task_000000_3a4d5e45
